commands.author: Jhacarreiro

target.title: Add OpenAI-compatible repair backend
target.body:
## Summary
- add a configurable repair model backend abstraction
- add an OpenAI-compatible repair backend with textual tool-call parsing and local result normalization
- add evidence-pack construction for source PR refs and repair signals
- document safe model backend config and include a gitignored example config path

## Security boundary updates after review
- `--sandbox read-only` is now enforced by the OpenAI-compatible runner: only `read_file`, `read_file_range`, `search_files`, and `git_diff` are exposed.
- write-capable tools (`write_file`, `replace_in_file`, `apply_patch`) and `run_command` are not exposed in read-only mode, and disallowed tool calls return a sandbox error.
- `GH_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` are no longer restored into the model-controlled OpenAI-compatible tool environment.
- steerable Codex app-server routing is now only used for the Codex CLI backend; selected non-Codex backends route through the backend command path instead of being silently ignored.

## Independence from #290
This PR was cherry-picked cleanly onto current upstream/main and does not include the repair-only PR intake commits from #290.

## Pre-publication checks
- searched for existing upstream PRs around OpenAI-compatible/model-backend/repair backend; no duplicate PR was found
- sanitized diff for local-only names, paths, hosts, private services, and secrets
- based on upstream/main 7779e2fe93

## Verification
- `pnpm run build:repair`
- `node --test test/repair/model-backend-config.test.ts test/repair/openai-compatible-runner-source.test.ts test/repair/openai-compatible-normalize-result.test.ts test/repair/openai-compatible-textual-tools.test.ts test/repair/run-worker.test.ts`

Result: 25 tests passed.

## Real OpenAI-compatible backend proof

Provider proof was run against OpenRouter's OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API with a redacted environment. No API key value was printed.

Read-only tool-loop proof:

```text
env CLAWSWEEPER_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 \
    CLAWSWEEPER_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_MODEL=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite \
    CLAWSWEEPER_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_API_KEY_ENV=OPENROUTER_API_KEY \
    CLAWSWEEPER_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_MAX_TURNS=2 \
    node dist/repair/openai-compatible-tools-runner.js \
      --cd <tmp-checkout> --sandbox read-only --output-last-message <tmp>/out.txt
```

Output excerpt:

```text
[openai-compatible-tools] chat_start turn=1 attempt=1/1 messages=2 bytes=3444 timeout_ms=30000 max_tokens=provider_default
[openai-compatible-tools] chat_done turn=1 attempt=1/1 status=200 elapsed_ms=1083 response_bytes=1179
[openai-compatible-tools] turn=1 tool_calls=1
tool_call: git_diff {}
tool_result: {"ok":true,"status":"","diff":""}
[openai-compatible-tools] chat_start turn=2 attempt=1/1 messages=4 bytes=3982 timeout_ms=30000 max_tokens=provider_default
[openai-compatible-tools] chat_done turn=2 attempt=1/1 status=200 elapsed_ms=1142 response_bytes=1052
assistant:
The repository is clean with no pending changes.
[openai-compatible-tools] turn=2 tool_calls=0
RUNNER_FINAL_DIFF_EXISTS=0
```

Schema-result proof:

```text
node dist/repair/openai-compatible-tools-runner.js \
  --cd <tmp-checkout> \
  --sandbox read-only \
  --output-schema schema/repair/codex-result.schema.json \
  --output-last-message <tmp>/result.json
```

Output excerpt:

```text
[openai-compatible-tools] chat_start turn=1 attempt=1/1 messages=2 bytes=3992 timeout_ms=30000 max_tokens=provider_default
[openai-compatible-tools] chat_done turn=1 attempt=1/1 status=200 elapsed_ms=3524 response_bytes=1529
assistant:
{
  "status": "needs_human",
  "repo": "openclaw/clawsweeper",
  "cluster_id": "repair-pr-openclaw-clawsweeper-292",
  "mode": "plan",
  "merge_preflight": [],
  "fix_artifact": null,
  "needs_human": {
    "action": "The current environment is a read-only sandbox without access to the source PR or repository metadata required to identify the repair signal. Please provide the PR context or repository access to proceed."
  }
}
[openai-compatible-tools] turn=1 tool_calls=0
RUNNER_FINAL_DIFF_EXISTS=0
```

The runner normalized the non-conforming provider JSON into a schema-valid repair result:

```json
{
  "status": "needs_human",
  "repo": "unknown/unknown",
  "cluster_id": "openai-compatible-fallback",
  "mode": "autonomous",
  "summary": "OpenAI-compatible worker returned non-conforming structured output.",
  "needs_human": [
    "{\"action\":\"The current environment is a read-only sandbox without access to the source PR or repository metadata required to identify the repair signal. Please provide the PR context or repository access to proceed.\"}"
  ],
  "actions": 1
}
```
target.author: jhacarreiro

commands.reason: re-review requires an open issue or PR

commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because this new backend changes repair execution security boundaries and lacks real backend proof; no automated repair marker should run until contributor proof is supplied.; Needs attention: The diff introduces concrete security-boundary concerns by restoring GitHub credentials to a model-controlled runner and ignoring the existing read-only sandbox contract.; Review finding: [P1] Hon... (sha=34c6f3ae2f06b5982c44894274e940cf8361935d)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a configurable repair model backend, an OpenAI-compatible tool-loop runner, repair evidence-pack generation, docs, and focused repair backend tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR findings: source inspection shows the new runner exposes mutating tools while run-worker still passes the read-only sandbox argument, and modelBackendEnv restores GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN. I did not run a live provider repro because the review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this new backend changes repair execution security boundaries and lacks real backend proof; no automated repair marker should run until contributor proof is supplied.
